summary: Couling iteration 2 — message-management features. Backend: 14 NEW pytest tests for delete-for-me, delete-for-everyone, clear-conversation, disappearing-messages (incl. forced-age auto-purge) all PASS; full suite 35/35 green against public ingress. Frontend: end-to-end mobile Playwright (390x844) verified splash → phone → OTP 123456 → profile → Circle → add Bobby (seeded via API) → open chat → send messages → long-press bubble shows action sheet with Copy / Delete-for-me / Delete-for-everyone → tapping Delete-for-everyone renders dashed-border tombstone bubble with 'This message was deleted'. Header ⋮ menu opens disappearing-messages sheet with 24h/7d/30d presets + custom minutes input; selecting 24h updates header chip to '1d' and privacy bar to 'Messages disappear after 1d · End-to-end secured'.

issues: react-native-web shadow* deprecation warnings persist (carried over from iter1; cosmetic only).

react_native_issues.issue: Both flows depend on RN core Alert.alert() for confirmation. On react-native-web Alert.alert does not always render a visible modal — the dialog is non-interactive in browser preview, so on web the Clear-conversation and swipe-confirm prompts may silently no-op. On real Expo Go / iOS / Android this works as designed. Optional improvement: replace Alert.alert with the existing in-app <Modal> pattern (already used for action-sheet & header menu) for cross-platform parity.

action_items: (Optional) Replace Alert.alert() confirmations in chat/[id].tsx with the existing <Modal>-based pattern so swipe-to-delete and Clear-conversation work identically on web preview as on native devices.
(Optional, carried over) Migrate deprecated shadow* style props → boxShadow in stylesheets to silence RN-Web warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: Delete-for-me uses set-union for hidden_for and physically purges the doc when both participants have hidden it — correct & idempotent. Subsequent DELETE on a purged msg returns 404 as expected.

Delete-for-everyone correctly enforces sender-only (403 for non-sender), and keeps the tombstone document with deleted_for_all=true, text='', deleted_at=now — UI uses these fields to render the 'This message was deleted' bubble.

_purge_expired runs on every GET /messages and physically delete_many's old docs based on chat.disappearing_seconds. Verified via a forced-age test (rewrote created_at to 120s in past with a 30s TTL → message purged for both users).

POST /clear writes cleared_at[user]=now, so per-user filtering in GET excludes earlier messages while the OTHER participant still sees them. When both users have cleared, oldest-cleared timestamp is used to physically delete the docs.
Disappearing endpoint correctly rejects seconds<30 and >1 year with 400. Null value disables the timer.
server.py is now 645 lines — still under the 700-line refactor threshold but trending up; consider splitting into routers (auth/contacts/chats/messages/calls/meetings) before the next feature batch.
No MongoDB ObjectId leakage anywhere — all queries use {_id: 0} projection consistently. Phone-number privacy preserved across all message-management endpoints (none of them echo phone numbers).

Frontend uses Pressable.onLongPress with delayLongPress=280 + Modal-based action sheet (no Alert.alert) — this works reliably on RN-Web. Good pattern; recommend extending to Clear/Swipe confirmations.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested chat UX flows (long-press action sheet, tombstone render, disappearing chip + privacy bar, custom-timer input, send/receive)

seed_data_creation: Each Playwright run creates a fresh Alice (phone +1555xxxxxxx) and Bob (also fresh phone, seeded via JS fetch from inside the page). pytest creates ephemeral users with +1888 prefix per run. No persistent seed data added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL: https://hidden-connect-12.preview.emergentagent.com. Universal OTP 123456. Frontend env: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Chat-screen testIDs: header-menu-btn, header-timer-chip, menu-disappearing, menu-clear, timer-{seconds} (e.g. timer-86400 / timer-604800 / timer-2592000 / timer-off), custom-minutes-input, apply-custom-timer, action-copy, action-delete-me, action-delete-everyone, message-{id}, message-input, send-btn. To open the action sheet via Playwright: locate the visible bubble via page.get_by_text(...).first (the testID message-{id} is duplicated on both the Swipeable wrapper and the inner Pressable, so query_selector_all returns multiple per message — pick by visible bounding box or by text). Long-press = mouse.down → wait ≥800ms → mouse.up. RN-Web Alert.alert does not render a clickable dialog, so the swipe-to-delete and Clear-conversation confirmation UIs cannot be exercised via web Playwright — verify those flows via the backend test_message_management.py suite or on a real device.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no functional defects. Only an environment-limitation note about Alert.alert on react-native-web (documented above as optional follow-up).
